secondary_archetype: Program Manager with Workplace / Real Estate focus

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in strategy and operations, management consulting, corporate real estate strategy, or a data-driven operational role
Leads cross-functional projects and drives operating cadences and governance forums

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in strategy and operations, management consulting, corporate real estate strategy, or a data-driven operational role in a fast-growing environment
Strong quantitative skills with a track record of using data to drive complex business cases and macro-level decisions
Demonstrated experience leading large-scale, cross-functional projects from ambiguous conception to rigorous execution
Experience working directly within or alongside Workplace or Real Estate teams

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Great collaboration and people skills, with the ability to build relationships with both leaders and individual contributors across the broader organization
High judgment with the ability to balance speed, risk, and organizational health
An inclination to solve problems systematically via infrastructure and automation
A clear willingness to take a hands-on approach and dive into the details
The ability to work independently or with a small team
Flexibility with changing requirements in an evolving and fast-paced environment
Strong communication skills and an ability to build and leverage cross-functional relationships
Ability to capture complex concepts and communicate them succinctly to key stakeholders

scope.surface: Workplace team — Real Estate Strategy & Transactions, Environments (design and construction), Experience (workplace services and programs)
scope.business_model: internal operations / corporate function at a B2B fintech
core_responsibilities: Assist the Head of Workplace in defining SOKRs and ensuring all pillars and cross-functional partners are aligned with the team's roadmap
Lead cross-functional projects that don't fit neatly into one department. Support M&A integrations
Identify bottlenecks in our current workflows and design scalable processes to remove them. Design and lead operating cadences and governance forums to ensure speed, clarity, and accountability

Conduct deep-dive analyses on business metrics to provide the Workplace Leadership Team with actionable recommendations for growth and efficiency. Drive the Workplace AI and data strategy in partnership with our IT Business Partner. Identify and implement innovative Workplace technologies and systems

green_flags: Reports directly to Head of Workplace — meaningful senior exposure and influence
Clear scope across three major Workplace pillars with high strategic visibility
Role has genuine cross-functional remit including M&A and AI/data strategy
JD distinguishes minimum requirements from preferred qualifications, encouraging applications from those who meet minimums
red_flags: No location, compensation, or visa/sponsorship information provided — candidates cannot assess fit on those dimensions
Role title says 'Manager' but JD does not explicitly state whether people management is expected — ambiguous
Preferred qualifications section is mostly soft/cultural traits, making it harder to self-assess differentiation

seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct people management language, but not explicitly excluded)

ambiguities: No office location or city is mentioned anywhere in the JD — it is unclear where this role is based or whether remote work is permitted.
